figures.caption: Figure 1: Initial collocation layout for NACA 65-1010. Orange dots are interior Sobol points; coloured dots match the tags in Table 1.
Table 1: Collocation-point budget and placement rules (per airfoil).
Figure 3: Spatial distribution of the geometry-aware weight field (case 65-1010). Hottest zones hug the blade and channel walls; the core region remains nearly unweighted.
Figure 8: CL vs. design lift m/10. PINN points are connected per (α, pout); CFD is shown as “×” without lines. NACA1010 with AoA 0 doesn’t have a steady state Euler solution
Figure 2: Main chain with special branch: 0610 to 1010 via 5° node.

Figure 6: Surface pressure coefficient Cp for NACA65-1010 (α = 0◦, pout = 0.8bar). PINN (solid blue) and CFD (dashed black) for pressure side (positive Cp) and suction side (negative Cp). large errors appear only near the leading edge and trailing edge where |Cp| approaches zero.
Table 2: Design matrix. All 20 combinations are solved with PINNs; the 12 starred (★) cases are additionally simulated with the CFD reference solver.
Figure 7: Training loss. Every loss bump refer to a boundary condition restart.
Figure 4: Velocity contours for the three training strategies. Cold start is non-physical; warm-start only under-predicts near the blade; combined schedule matches CFD closely.
